headline: Insurance Penetration Stalls at 55% Leaving 13M Unprotected
context: Bank Negara's 2025 Financial Stability Review showed life insurance and family takaful penetration at 55%, unchanged from 2020, leaving 13 million working-age Malaysians without any life or medical coverage.

cards.big: 13 million Malaysians have zero life insurance — one medical emergency can bankrupt an unprotected family
cards.sub: Average hospital bill for heart surgery is RM65,000 — exceeding 3 years of B40 household savings

cards.big: Insurance penetration at 55% is below Thailand's 68% and Singapore's 82% despite Malaysia's higher GDP per capita
cards.sub: Industry spends RM4.2 billion annually on agent commissions but only RM180 million on financial literacy programmes

cards.big: B40 insurance ownership at 28% versus T20 at 92% — protection gap is fundamentally an income inequality issue
cards.sub: Minimum life insurance premium of RM120 monthly is 8% of B40 income — unaffordable without subsidy

cards.big: MySalam national health protection scheme covers only critical illness — not hospitalisation, disability, or income loss
cards.sub: BNM's Perlindungan Tenang products designed for B40 saw only 340,000 policies sold against 8 million target

cards.big: When half the population can't afford insurance, the problem isn't financial illiteracy — it's that protection is priced for the wealthy
Should Malaysia introduce mandatory basic life and medical insurance funded through a 0.5% payroll levy for all employers?
